name: Guadiana Nature Trail Loop
name_original: Cañada Real Soriana hasta el Puente de las Ovejas — circular desde Los Pozuelos de Calatrava
description: Hike the 7.2-mile Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas trail from Los Pozuelos de Calatrava, exploring historic drovers' roads.
region_intro: Hiking around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava offers routes through diverse landscapes, characterized by river valleys and gentle elevations. The region features trails alongside the Bullaque and Guadiana rivers, providing opportunities for tranquil walks. Hikers can explore areas with historical significance, such as routes leading to ancient castles. The terrain is generally accessible, with options ranging from easy riverside paths to moderate trails with some ascent. Best hiking trails around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava The most popular hiking route is Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This easy circular path follows the Río Bullaque. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino Natural del Guadiana — circular desde Luciana, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path. This route offers a circular experience along the Guadiana River from Luciana. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Castillo de Caracuel de Calatrava — circular from Corral de Calatrava, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with an ascent to a historic castle, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava is defined by river valleys, gentle hills, and historical sites, with routes offering options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 4 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Pozuelos De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: There are nearly 20 hiking trails around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava, offering a variety of experiences. Most routes are moderate, but there are also several easy options for a more relaxed walk.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in the area?
A: Yes, Los Pozuelos De Calatrava offers several easy trails suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094013256/rio-bullaque-circular-por-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.7 km) circular path that follows the tranquil Río Bullaque.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: Many of the trails in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198546491/camino-natural-del-guadiana-circular-desde-luciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Guadiana — circular desde Luciana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198481191/ascent-to-castillo-de-caracuel-de-calatrava-circular-from-corral-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Castillo de Caracuel de Calatrava — circular from Corral de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is characterized by its river valleys. Along the trails, you can discover natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4017346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorge Narrows of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the area has historical significance. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198481191/ascent-to-castillo-de-caracuel-de-calatrava-circular-from-corral-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Caracuel de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, a historic castle. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sheep Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3762521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geldres Mill&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: The region's climate generally allows for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for exploring the river valleys and gentle elevations. Winter hiking is also possible, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: The trails in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the tranquil riverside paths, the diverse landscapes, and the opportunities to discover historical sites.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Given the presence of river valleys like the Bullaque and Guadiana, the area provides habitats for various wildlife. Keep an eye out for birds and other small animals, especially along the quieter riverside sections of the trails.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, the terrain is generally accessible, with options ranging from easy riverside paths to moderate trails. Beginners can comfortably enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094013256/rio-bullaque-circular-por-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, which is classified as easy.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, for scenic vistas, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views of the surrounding landscape and is accessible from some of the local trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hikes?
A: The terrain around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava is diverse, featuring river valleys, gentle elevations, and some varied paths. You'll find everything from flat riverside walks to moderate ascents, particularly on trails leading to historical sites like castles.
tips:
Located in the central part of the province, the surroundings of Los Pozuelos de Calatrava are characterized by presenting a soft relief characteristic of the Campo de Calatrava region. It is crossed from east to west by the Guadiana River and streams such as Fresno.
Regarding its heritage, the Church of Our Lady of the Visitation stands out.

Also called the thousand sheep or Merinas bridge, it was built to count the sheep that came from the north to the Alcudia valley.

For using the same, the shepherds had to pay a tribute called “Portazgo”.

Very close, just 3 kilometers from there, we can visit the Martinete, an iron foundry from the 19th century.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Los Pozuelos de Calatrava. You can typically find parking within the village itself, as it serves as the primary access point for this hike.

faqs.question: Is the Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as much of the route traverses livestock pastures, it's essential to keep dogs on a leash to ensure the safety and comfort of both your pet and the grazing animals.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer pleasant weather for walking and showcase the diverse colors of the landscape, from blooming wildflowers to autumnal hues in the olive groves and vineyards.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas trail. It follows public rights of way, including the historic drovers' road.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While not overly strenuous, its length of 11.6 kilometers and varied terrain require a good level of fitness and stamina. It's suitable for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, including expansive farmland, moorlands, and meadows. The route features olive groves, vineyards, Mediterranean scrubland, and reddish marl soils. Along the Guadiana River, you'll find magnificent ash trees providing shade. The terrain is varied, following sections of the ancient Cañada Real Soriana.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the historic Los Pozuelos de Calatrava, where the trail begins and ends, and the significant Sheep Bridge (Puente de las Ovejas) over the Guadiana River, known for its unique design to count sheep during transhumance. The trail also passes through a Special Area of Conservation (ZEC) and a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA).

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this trail overlap with other official routes. Approximately 66% of the route follows the Camino Natural del Guadiana, and about 27% aligns with the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental.

faqs.question: Is this a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cañada Real Soriana to Puente de las Ovejas is a circular trail, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Los Pozuelos de Calatrava.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail passes through a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA) and a Special Area of Conservation (ZEC) within the Guadiana River basin, you have a good chance of spotting various bird species. The area is also a natural corridor for other wildlife, though specific sightings can vary.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Los Pozuelos de Calatrava, you can find local amenities such as cafes or restaurants in the village for refreshments before or after your hike.
